summary: chai typosquat fetches and executes remote JavaScript on import
details: chai-utils-test impersonates the popular `chai` assertion library (copying chai's description, keywords, and contributor list verbatim in package.json) and ships a remote-code loader. The package's main entry index.js unconditionally calls validate_assert() at top level, which uses child_process.spawn to launch a detached, stdio-ignored, unref'd Node subprocess running lib/chai/utils/assertion.js. That script fetches JavaScript from https://statecheck.ddns.net/api/scanner.js (a dynamic-DNS host that the operator can mutate at any time) and executes the response via `new Function('require', s)(require)`, handing the attacker arbitrary code execution with full Node `require` access in the installer's environment. The detached/unref pattern is designed so the malicious child outlives the importing process. Any consumer that runs `require('chai-utils-test')` — including a developer who mistypes `chai` — triggers the fetch-and-execute chain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
